title: kermadecia ferruginea
intro: Kermadecia ferruginea, also known as the ironwood or rusty nut tree, is a flowering plant species native to the Pacific island region, specifically in the Kermadec Islands and New Caledonia. The plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Kermadecia ferruginea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into a paste, which is applied topically to affected areas, or infused as a tea to support overall well-being.
